questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Consider the function $\mathrm{y}=\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})$ $\mathrm{f}: \mathrm{R}-\{0\} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}$ The functional rule for the function $y=f(x)$ is same as that of the functional rule for hypotenuse ' $h$ ' of the right triangle with area 25 (units) ${ }^2$ expressed as a function of its perimeter. On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question The sum of all the values of $x$ at which fof $(\mathrm{x})$ is discontinuous -
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{2} a b=25 \Rightarrow a b=50 \\ & a+b+h=x \\ & h=x-(a+b) \end{aligned}$ Now, $\mathrm{a}^2+\mathrm{b}^2=\mathrm{h}^2$
$\begin{aligned} & (a+b)^2-2 a b=h^2 \\ & (x-h)^2-100=h^2 \\ & x^2-2 x h+h^2-100=h^2 \\ & h=\frac{x^2-100}{2 x}\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & f(\mathrm{x})=\frac{\mathrm{x}^2-100}{2 \mathrm{x}} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad f \text { is discontinuous at } \mathrm{x}=0 \\ & f(f(\mathrm{x}))=\frac{\mathrm{f}^2(\mathrm{x})-100}{2 \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})}\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad f(f(x)) \text { is discontinuous at all } x, \\ & \quad \text { where } f(x)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{x^2-100}{2 x}=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x= \pm 10 \end{aligned}$ Sum of values of x where $f(f(\mathrm{x}))$ is discontinuous is $0+10-10=0$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: PARAGRAPH: Let $g(x)= \begin{cases}\frac{a x^2+b x+c\left(\cot ^n x\right)}{4+\cot ^n x}, & x \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{4}\right) \\ 1, & x=\frac{\pi}{4} \\ \frac{\sin x+\cos x+\tan ^n x}{1+c\left(\tan ^n x\right)}, & x \in\left(\frac{\pi}{4}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)\end{cases}$ where $a, b, c$ are real constants, and $f(x)=\operatorname{Lim}_{n \rightarrow \infty} g(x)$QUESTION: If $f(x)$ is continuous at $x=\frac{\pi}{4}$ then the value of $f\left(\frac{\pi}{6}\right)+f\left(\frac{\pi}{3}\right)$ is
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f(x)=\left(a x^2+b x+c\right) \operatorname{sgn}(2 \sin x-1)$ be a continuous function $\forall x \in(0,6)$ where $a, b, c \in R$. [Note: sgn (.) represents signum function.]Question: If $a \neq 0$, then which of the following must be incorrect?
options: $y=f(x)$ is not differentiable at exactly two points
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& f(x)=\left(a x^2+b x+c\right) \operatorname{sgn}(2 \sin x-1) \\
& f(x)=-\left(a x^2+b x+c\right), 0 < x < \frac{\pi}{6} \\
& f(x)=0, x=\frac{\pi}{6} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad f(x)=\left(a x^2+b x+c\right), \frac{\pi}{6} < x < \frac{5 \pi}{6} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad f(x)=0, x=\frac{5 \pi}{6}
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\Rightarrow f(x)=-\left(a x^2+b x+c\right), \frac{5 \pi}{6} < x < 6
$$

If $y=f(x)$ is continuous, then $a x^2+b x+c=a\left(x-\frac{\pi}{6}\right)\left(x-\frac{5 \pi}{6}\right)$
If $c=0 \Rightarrow a=b=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Consider the function $\mathrm{y}=\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})$ $\mathrm{f}: \mathrm{R}-\{0\} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}$ The functional rule for the function $y=f(x)$ is same as that of the functional rule for hypotenuse ' $h$ ' of the right triangle with area 25 (units) ${ }^2$ expressed as a function of its perimeter. On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question The value of $\left[\cos ^{-1} \cos f\left(\left(2 \log _2 3 \cdot \log _3 4 \log _4 5 \ldots \ldots . \log _{31} 32\right)+10\right)\right]$, where $[\cdot]$ denotes the greatest integer function -
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{2} a b=25 \Rightarrow a b=50 \\ & a+b+h=x \\ & h=x-(a+b) \end{aligned}$ Now, $\mathrm{a}^2+\mathrm{b}^2=\mathrm{h}^2$
$\begin{aligned} & (a+b)^2-2 a b=h^2 \\ & (x-h)^2-100=h^2 \\ & x^2-2 x h+h^2-100=h^2 \\ & h=\frac{x^2-100}{2 x}\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & 2 \cdot \log _2 3 \cdot \log _3 4 \ldots \ldots . \log _{31} 32 \\ &= \frac{2 \cdot \log 3}{\log 2} \frac{\log 4}{\log 3} \ldots \ldots \cdot \cdot \frac{\log 32}{\log 31}=2 \cdot \frac{\log 32}{\log 2} \\ &= 2 \cdot 5=10 \\ & \cos ^{-1} \cos (f(10)+10)=\cos ^{-1} \cos 10 \\ &(\because \mathrm{f}(10)=0) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad[4 \pi-10]=2 .\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: PARAGRAPH: Let $g(x)= \begin{cases}\frac{a x^2+b x+c\left(\cot ^n x\right)}{4+\cot ^n x}, & x \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{4}\right) \\ 1, & x=\frac{\pi}{4} \\ \frac{\sin x+\cos x+\tan ^n x}{1+c\left(\tan ^n x\right)}, & x \in\left(\frac{\pi}{4}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)\end{cases}$ where $a, b, c$ are real constants, and $f(x)=\operatorname{Lim}_{n \rightarrow \infty} g(x)$QUESTION: If $f(x)$ is continuous at $x=\frac{\pi}{4}$ then c is equal to
questions.solution:
Clearly $\mathrm{f}\left(\frac{\pi^{-}}{4}\right)=\mathrm{c} ; \quad \mathrm{f}\left(\frac{\pi^{+}}{4}\right)=\frac{1}{\mathrm{c}}$
(i) As $\operatorname{Lim}_{x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{4}} f(x)$ exists, so $c=\frac{1}{c} \Rightarrow c= \pm 1$
(ii) As $f(x)$ is continuous at $x=\frac{\pi}{4}$, so $\operatorname{Lim}_{x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{4}} f(x)=f\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \Rightarrow c=\frac{1}{c}=1 \Rightarrow c=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f(x)=\left(a x^2+b x+c\right) \operatorname{sgn}(2 \sin x-1)$ be a continuous function $\forall x \in(0,6)$ where $a, b, c \in R$. [Note: sgn (.) represents signum function.]Question: If $c=0$, then $(a+b)$ equals:
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& f(x)=\left(a x^2+b x+c\right) \operatorname{sgn}(2 \sin x-1) \\
& f(x)=-\left(a x^2+b x+c\right), 0 < x < \frac{\pi}{6} \\
& f(x)=0, x=\frac{\pi}{6} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad f(x)=\left(a x^2+b x+c\right), \frac{\pi}{6} < x < \frac{5 \pi}{6} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad f(x)=0, x=\frac{5 \pi}{6}
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\Rightarrow f(x)=-\left(a x^2+b x+c\right), \frac{5 \pi}{6} < x < 6
$$

If $y=f(x)$ is continuous, then $a x^2+b x+c=a\left(x-\frac{\pi}{6}\right)\left(x-\frac{5 \pi}{6}\right)$
If $c=0 \Rightarrow a=b=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f$ be a differentiable function satisfying $$ \sqrt[3]{f(x+y)}=\sqrt[3]{f(x)}+\sqrt[3]{f(y)}+1 \forall x, y \in R \text { and } f^{\prime}(0)=3 $$Question: If $h(x)=f(x)-x^3$, then number of point(s) where $y=h(|x|)$ is non-derivable is(are):
questions.solution:
$$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=(x-1)^3 \\ & f(x)-x^3=-3 x^2+3 x-1=h(x) \end{aligned} $$ (i) Clearly, $y=h(|x|)$ is non-derivable at exactly one point.
(ii) $\quad f(x)$ and $f^{-1}(x)$ meet on the line $y=x$ at $x=x_0$ where $2 < x_0 < 3$. $\{\because \quad f(2)=1$ and $f(3)=8\}$
$$ \therefore \quad \cos ^{-1}\left(\cos 2 x_0\right)+4 \tan ^{-1}\left(\tan \frac{x_0}{2}\right)=\left(2 \pi-2 x_0\right)+4 \frac{x_0}{2}=2 \pi $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f$ be a differentiable function satisfying $$ \sqrt[3]{f(x+y)}=\sqrt[3]{f(x)}+\sqrt[3]{f(y)}+1 \forall x, y \in R \text { and } f^{\prime}(0)=3 $$Question: If $x_0$ is solution of the equation $f(x)=f^{-1}(x)$, then $\cos ^{-1}\left(\cos 2 x_0\right)+4 \tan ^{-1}\left(\tan \frac{x_0}{2}\right)$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
$$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=(x-1)^3 \\ & f(x)-x^3=-3 x^2+3 x-1=h(x) \end{aligned} $$ (i) Clearly, $y=h(|x|)$ is non-derivable at exactly one point.
(ii) $\quad f(x)$ and $f^{-1}(x)$ meet on the line $y=x$ at $x=x_0$ where $2 < x_0 < 3$. $\{\because \quad f(2)=1$ and $f(3)=8\}$
$$ \therefore \quad \cos ^{-1}\left(\cos 2 x_0\right)+4 \tan ^{-1}\left(\tan \frac{x_0}{2}\right)=\left(2 \pi-2 x_0\right)+4 \frac{x_0}{2}=2 \pi $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
